UTF-8 Encoding Corruption via PowerShell Set-Content

PowerShell's Set-Content command defaults to system encoding (Windows-1252) instead of UTF-8. When used to inject JavaScript or HTML strings containing em dashes (—) or emoji characters, it converted them to garbled sequences: — became â€  and emoji became ðŸ garbage strings."
Claude instructed Tony to use PowerShell string injection to add the setMode() kill switch JavaScript function and favicon base64 data into dashboard.html.

Base64 Favicon Injection Breaking APPS Tab

A very long base64 string was injected into line 8 of dashboard.html via PowerShell. This caused downstream HTML parsing issues that broke the APPS+ tab — clicking it showed nothing. The base64 string itself was clean but the injection method disrupted the file structure.
Attempt to embed favicon.ico as inline base64 data URI so Azure could display the tab icon without needing a server route.

Git Push ≠ Azure Deploy (Disconnected Chain)

Git push to GitHub (github.com/decryptingmymind/AlgoTradez.git) does NOT auto-deploy to Azure. Azure was serving a completely separate copy of the files uploaded originally via az webapp up. Every git push during the session had zero effect on the live site.
Assumed that az webapp up (original deploy) created a GitHub-connected deployment. It did not. az webapp deployment source show confirmed: branch: null / repoUrl: null.
All — entire site was out of sync
az webapp deployment source show --name algotradez-aios confirmed no GitHub connection. Dashboard still showed MAMA NODE after multiple git pushes.
Any changes made via VS Code + git push were invisible to Azure. The live site was always serving the original uploaded copy, never the updated version.
az webapp restart Does Not Clear Azure Cache
Running az webapp restart after a deploy does not guarantee Azure serves the new files. Azure caches aggressively. The dashboard showed MAMA NODE instead of AI-OS Node even after multiple restarts.
Claude used az webapp restart as the deploy confirmation step throughout the session.
Full stop/start cycle: az webapp stop → Start-Sleep 5 → az webapp start. This forces full cache clear.
ENFORCED RULE saved to memory: Never use az webapp restart for production deploys. Always use stop/start.
Zip Deploy Including Conflicting index.html Files

When the full ai_Os folder was zipped and deployed, it included apps/dashboard/index.html (a React app scaffold) alongside the root index.html redirect. Azure got confused about which file to serve as the entry point, causing permission errors and no-CSS rendering.
Compress-Archive with wildcard (*) captured all subfolders including the apps/ React scaffold.

AI-OS Node is a live financial dashboard connected to a real OANDA practice trading account. Corruption or downtime directly impacts visibility into Trading Engine V1 signals and trade execution. As the system approaches production with real capital ($100K target), zero-tolerance for deploy errors is required.

Local (VS Code edit) → Compress-Archive (selective zip) → az webapp deploy (zip type) → Azure App Service (Python 3.11 / B1 SKU) → served via Flask app.py → browser at algotradez-aios.azurewebsites.net
Local repo at C:\Users\mylab\OneDrive\Desktop\ai_Os → GitHub (decryptingmymind/AlgoTradez) — version history and backup ONLY. Not connected to Azure auto-deploy.

Azure App Service with Python runtime needs a WSGI entry point. Without app.py, Azure doesn't know how to serve HTML files with correct Content-Type headers. This causes browsers to ignore inline CSS.
